title: Microsoft reportedly leans more on in-house models to cut AI costs
friendlyTitle: Microsoft may use more of its own AI models to save money
summary: TechCrunch reports that Microsoft is relying more on its own AI models in some products as companies look for ways to control rising AI costs. The article frames the move as part of a broader industry push to optimize model choice instead of defaulting to the most expensive frontier providers.
friendlySummary: Microsoft is reportedly using more in-house AI models in some products as AI costs keep rising.
whyItMatters: AI spending is becoming a product and procurement discipline. For users and businesses, expect more tools to route tasks across model tiers, balancing quality, latency, privacy, and price instead of treating one model as the answer to everything.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This means AI apps may start choosing cheaper or smaller models for many tasks. That could make tools more affordable, but quality will need watching.
